title: AgentFinding
description: One observed-vulnerable behavioral test on an agent scan (an `agent_findings` row). Findings rows are `verdict='vulnerable'`; the `not_observed`/`n_a`/`error` outcomes live on the report `checks` array (proof-of-tests), not here. Hash/slot-only evidence — NEVER raw agent output per .claude/rules/security.md § Scan-trace transparency. The explainable-finding prose (title/explanation/severityRationale/remediation) is NOT a column — it is pack-sourced and inlined by the report builder at request time, exactly as report_builder.py inlines component rule prose. The post-redaction transcript window (`evidenceExcerpt`) is report-DTO-only, hydrated only on the unlisted token route.

agentRunId.description: FK to the parent agent_runs row.

testId.description: Behavioral test id (AS-01…AS-22) — keys the pack-sourced prose join.

severity.description: 5-tier severity ladder, reused from the component engine.

verdict.description: Per-test outcome. `agent_findings` rows are always `vulnerable`; the other outcomes live on the report `checks` array.

family.description: OWASP family label used for grouping (proof-of-tests tally).

owaspRefs.description: OWASP ids this finding maps to, e.g. ["ASI01:2026","LLM01:2025"].

scoreDelta.description: Signed penalty this finding contributed to the behavioral score (≤0). Drives the 'How the score moved' table.

leakedCanarySlot.description: Which canary slot leaked — the slot id ONLY, never the raw canary value.

evidenceExcerpt.description: Report-DTO-only post-redaction transcript window with the leaked canary highlighted. Resolved at request time from the raw-evidence store; null on the public projection. NOT a column and NOT a scan-trace field (the findings table stays hash/slot-only).
